creator: eric@madrid.lol
text:
(In reply to Mark Linimon from comment #1)

Ok, I figured out how to do ports...

The "make install" brakes the php-fpm service it will not restart.
If I "make deinstall" and run the "pkg install php73-pecl-mongodb".
The service will restart but I am back to where I started.

I finally figured out how to update my pkg, "pkg update" does not do it.
I have to "pkg install" each pkg to get them to upgrade.

My site: http://9d.ee:8093/index.php?ps=a
Now reads:

OS:		FreeBSD 11.2-RELEASE amd64
Web Server:	nginx/1.14.0  <------------------------ Update (1.14.0.x)
Server Side:	PHP 7.2.10  <-------------------------- Update (7.2.x)
Databases:	MongoDB NoSQL 3.6.6  <----------------- Update (3.6.x)
PHP 2 Mongo:	PECL classes for MongoDB 1.5.2  <------ Update (1.x.x)

I was afraid to update the php72-pecl-mongodb from 1.4.4 to the 1.5.2, but oh well if it does not work I give up on FreeBSD...

It worked...! I assume that it has something to do with the FreeBSD 11.2 vs FreeBSD 13. 

I would now put it in the bug category the 1.5.2 works fine in the FreeBSD 11.2 release.

Hope that helps.

Eric.
